heading: Judicial review; admissibility
label: 4015 Judicial review; admissibility
notes: Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as a note under .Oct. 8, 1982section 312 of Pub. L. 97–290section 4011 of this title

children.heading: District court review of grants or denials; erroneous determination
children.label: a District court review of grants or denials; erroneous determination
children.text: If the Secretary grants or denies, in whole or in part, an application for a certificate of review or for an amendment to a certificate, or revokes or modifies a certificate pursuant to , any person aggrieved by such determination may, within 30 days of the determination, bring an action in any appropriate district court of the United States to set aside the determination on the ground that such determination is erroneous.section 4014(b) of this title

children.heading: Exclusive provision for review
children.label: b Exclusive provision for review
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (a), no action by the Secretary or the Attorney General pursuant to this subchapter shall be subject to judicial review.

children.heading: Inadmissibility in antitrust proceedings
children.label: c Inadmissibility in antitrust proceedings
children.text: If the Secretary denies, in whole or in part, an application for a certificate of review or for an amendment to a certificate, or revokes or amends a certificate, neither the negative determination nor the statement of reasons therefor shall be admissible in evidence, in any administrative or judicial proceeding, in support of any claim under the antitrust laws.
